title: Sweet Smoky Turkish Lamb Lahmacun
description: A winey and cheesy Turkish dish featuring sugar syrup, perfect for a weeknight dinner, this Sweet Smoky Turkish Lamb Lahmacun is a twist on the traditional lahmacun recipe, with a sweet and smoky flavor profile
instructions: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat, about 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine the ground lamb, chopped onion, minced garlic, and chopped red bell pepper, mixing well with your hands until just combined.
Season the lamb mixture with smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper, and mix again until just combined.
Brush the pita bread pieces with olive oil on both sides.
Divide the lamb mixture among the pita bread pieces, spreading it evenly to the edges.
Top each pita bread piece with crumbly feta cheese.
Place the lahmacun on the preheated grill and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side, until the bread is toasted and the cheese is melted and slightly charred.
While the lahmacun is cooking, warm the sugar syrup in a small saucepan over low heat.
Once the lahmacun is cooked, brush the warm sugar syrup over the top of each piece.
Serve the Sweet Smoky Turkish Lamb Lahmacun immediately, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro if desired.
